pages.raw_text:
Five unions last night endorsed the
action of the trades council in recon-
mending walkout in Spokane of all
union workers within two weeks. UNI
less the gas company agrees to the
demands Of union labor.


The action of the trades council
was brought up before all the unions
that met last night at Central Labor
hall and everyone Of them decided to
stand by the recommendation of the
council. In particular the BARRIERS
union took q determined position.


Hardly had the meeting been called
to order than the members began to
ask for the consideration of the
strike question. President Otis ad-
vised the adoption Of conservative
policy, but the members were radical
in their views and wanted the matter
settled at once. Many addresses Of
the philippe kind were made, and
when the question was finally put the
meeting rose en- masse and with a
vociferous voice voted in the affirma-
tive.


You may say from me-" said Pres
ident Otis, that the BARRIERS union
Is going to help the gas workers to
fight this thing to a finish. We never
go back on the trades council. The
report that we refused to fall In line
IL absolutely false"


The plumbers and steamfltters urge
the fight even more than the gas
workers. The members of the ex-
ecutive board state that It is custom
airy for the union 20 refer all such
matters to them. Said Mr.. Dempsey:


''Of all unions in the city none will
enter the strike with more enthusiasm
than the plumbers. The action Of the
trades council was brought up for
consideration last night. In accord
ance with FORM. It was placed in the
hands of the executive committee and
the eTkautive committee will lendorse
1t. l am a member of that COM
mittee, as are all the other officers of
the union"
